title: Piquant, Velvety French Mung Bean Sprouts Bowl
description: A hearty, low-glycemic French-inspired bowl featuring tender mung bean sprouts, savory roasted vegetables, and a tangy, velvety sauce. Perfect for a comforting dinner or lunch.
instructions: Toss the sliced onion, chopped carrots, and chopped red bell pepper with 1 tablespoon of olive oil on a baking sheet. Roast in the preheated oven for 20 minutes, or until the vegetables are tender.
In a large skillet, heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil over medium heat. Add the sliced mushrooms and cook for 5 minutes, or until they release their moisture and start to brown.
Add the minced garlic to the skillet with the mushrooms and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the roasted vegetables, mung bean sprouts, burgundy wine, vegetable broth, Dijon mustard, tahini, and lemon juice to the skillet. Stir to combine.
Reduce heat to low and simmer the mixture for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sauce has thickened and the flavors have melded together.
Season the sauce with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve the piquant, velvety sauce over the mung bean sprouts in a large earthenware bowl.
Garnish with fresh herbs, if desired.
